mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect immediate capacity utilization gains and supply-side momentum, which trigger short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO momentum, or the signal reverses direction during high volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 70% of high-inflation months, or the predictive IC drops below zero after adjusting for bond yield movements.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms structural labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or positive returns do not materialize when payrolls rise alongside falling unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts indicate strong residential investment demand and construction sector activity, which directly boosts related equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST growth, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for mortgage rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals immediate consumer demand contraction, which forces earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of retail sales decline months, or the threshold signal loses predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.
